response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,2,10]],"date-time":"2026-02-10T21:21:57Z","timestamp":1770758517105,"version":"3.50.0"},"posted":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"group-title":"SSRN","reference-count":0,"publisher":"Elsevier BV","content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":[],"abstract":"<jats:p>The accurate classification of the skin lesions and the earlier date of detection are needed to improve patient outcomes and reduce mortality rates because of skin cancer. This paper presents a novel deep-learning approach that combines the fractional-derivative image enhancement technique with parallel convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to differentiate between malignant and benign lesions. We use multiple orders of the derivatives of a fractional representation, namely, 0.5, 0.8, 1.0, 1.2, and 1.6, to highlight textual characteristics on different spatial scales and then apply the outputs as inputs of parallel CNN branches with pre-trained DenseNet, ResNet, and VGG networks. Images are processed in each branch with a specific fractional order; thus, complementary, multiscale texture is captured, and finally, probabilistic averaging is performed. When we applied to the ISIC2019 dataset (which consists of 25,331 dermoscopic images), the proposed fractional-derivative-based parallel-CNN model achieves the accuracy of 94.9\\%, precision of 95.3\\%, recall of 94.1\\%, F1-score of 94.7\\% and AUC-ROC of 97.9\\%. Such measures greatly exceed current state-of-the-art methods, highlighting the effectiveness of fractional calculus in enhancing medical images. The multi-scale feature extraction can be used to produce complementary texture clues by changing the fractional orders, as they improve the strength of classification over a range of lesion types.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.2139\/ssrn.6146189","type":"posted-content","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,2,10]],"date-time":"2026-02-10T17:02:32Z","timestamp":1770742952000},"source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Skin Lesion Image Classification Based on Fractional Derivatives with Convolutional Neural Network"],"prefix":"10.2139","author":[{"given":"AHMAD","family":"DERBAS","sequence":"first","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Bur\u00e7in","family":"\u00d6zmen","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"78","container-title":[],"original-title":[],"deposited":{"date-parts":[[2026,2,10]],"date-time":"2026-02-10T17:02:32Z","timestamp":1770742952000},"score":1,"resource":{"primary":{"URL":"https:\/\/www.ssrn.com\/abstract=6146189"}},"subtitle":[],"short-title":[],"issued":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"references-count":0,"URL":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.2139\/ssrn.6146189","relation":{},"subject":[],"published":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"subtype":"preprint"}}
